From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

International handball tournament for men's national teams

This article is about the indoor championship. For the outdoor championship, see [IHF World Men's Field Handball Championship](/wiki/IHF_World_Men%27s_Field_Handball_Championship "IHF World Men's Field Handball Championship"). For the women's tournament, see [IHF World Women's Handball Championship](/wiki/IHF_World_Women%27s_Handball_Championship "IHF World Women's Handball Championship").

The **IHF Men's Handball World Championship** has been organized indoor by the [International Handball Federation](/wiki/International_Handball_Federation "International Handball Federation") since 1938.

In the twenty-nine tournaments held, twelve national teams have won the title. [France](/wiki/France_men%27s_national_handball_team "France men's national handball team") is the most successful team with six titles, followed by [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden_men%27s_national_handball_team "Sweden men's national handball team"), [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark_men%27s_national_handball_team "Denmark men's national handball team") and [Romania](/wiki/Romania_men%27s_national_handball_team "Romania men's national handball team") with four titles each.

The current champion is [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark_men%27s_national_handball_team "Denmark men's national handball team"), which won its fourth consecutive title at the 2025 tournament in Croatia, Denmark and Norway.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IHF_World_Men%27s_Handball_Championship&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The first indoor championship took place in Germany in 1938, involving four teams from Europe made up of 7 players who competed in a round robin stage to find a winner. It wouldn't be until sixteen years later where the second World Championship was held in the country of Sweden. Throughout their history, the World Championships have been dominated by European teams, with no medals being won by non-European countries until 2015, by Qatar. Over the years, the organization of the World Championships has changed. Initially, there were group games in both the preliminary and main rounds, but since the [1995 edition](/wiki/1995_World_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "1995 World Men's Handball Championship") a knockout system has been applied after the preliminary round.[[2]](#cite_note-IHF-2)

5. **[^](#cite_ref-YUG_7-0)** Competed as [SFR Yugoslavia](/wiki/Socialist_Federal_Republic_of_Yugoslavia "Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia") in 1958–1990.
6. **[^](#cite_ref-FRYUG_8-0)** Competed as [FR Yugoslavia](/wiki/Serbia_and_Montenegro "Serbia and Montenegro") in 1997–2003 and as [Serbia and Montenegro](/wiki/Serbia_and_Montenegro "Serbia and Montenegro") in 2005.
